name: Caramel Cage Covers for Ice Cream (Kh)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover the outside (rounded part) of a 6 oz ladle with aluminum foil, ensuring the surface is smooth and tight.
2. Lightly oil the domed surface of the foil.
3. In a saucepan, combine sugar, water, and cream of tartar. Mix well and cover.
4. Heat the mixture over medium-low heat until the sugar dissolves completely.
5. Continue cooking until the syrup reaches a light gold color (about 340 degrees F on a candy thermometer).
6. Remove the pan from the heat and let sit at room temperature for 6-8 minutes, allowing it to turn a darker gold color.
7. Hold the foil-covered ladle over the saucepan. Dip a spoon into the caramel mixture and drizzle over the ladle, swirling your hand to create a wedge or lattice pattern on the foil.
8. Prop up the ladle so the caramel cage cools completely (about 10 minutes).
9. Carefully pry the foil away from the ladle without breaking the fragile structure.
10. Trim any uneven strands with scissors to form a straight bottom.
11. Peel off the foil, and you have a beautiful candy cage for ice cream. Place it over a single scoop of ice cream and enjoy the special effect when tapped with the back of a spoon.
